response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web engineer with broad polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (Dec 2014–Jun 2015, ~6 months), but falls dramatically short of the 8-year C++ minimum. There is no demonstrated experience building large-scale cloud compute platforms, distributed systems infrastructure at the scale implied, or formal technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers. Career trajectory leans toward web/GIS/data integration rather than systems-level compute platform engineering.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with senior/staff titles and craftsmanship focus (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
Some distributed/high-throughput exposure (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of req/s in DLang)
Mentoring and teaching track record via workshops, dojos, and Selenium course
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience vs. 8-year hard requirement
No evidence of leading or architecting an internal cloud compute platform
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / large-scale infra leadership
Senior engineering breadth and mentoring history
